hypothesis: If an autonomous materials discovery agent in MADE uses the pretrained MatterGen generator with a diversity-based acquisition strategy, then within a 20-query budget it will discover significantly more novel stable compounds (ε ≤ 0.01 eV/atom) across Na–O and Co–Nb–Sn than agents using random generation and/or random acquisition because chemically informed generation combined with diversity-driven selection more efficiently explores high-yield regions of composition and structure space.
